title: Night
content:
Silence, and whirling worlds afar
    Through all encircling skies.
    What floods come o'er the spirit's bar,
    What wondrous thoughts arise.

    The earth, a mantle falls away,
    And, winged, we leave the sod;
    Where shines in its eternal sway
    The majesty of God.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Laurence Dunbar, titled "Night", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
